To ensure we address our customers' concerns effectively, we have established a variety of communication channels, including the Complaint Handling System (covering both administrative and technical matters), Customer Care, and our official website. Throughout the reporting year, we received a total of 49 administrative complaints and 100 technical complaints. As of March 31, 2025, we acknowledge that 4 administrative complaints (8%) and 12 technical complaints (12%) remain open and we are committed to resolving them promptly.

SKF India strives to meet the needs of our customers through 
innovative products and solutions.

SKF is a profit-driven organisation. The financial performance is the overall indicator of the economic impact SKF has on society, as an employer, a member of the stock market and a customer and supplier to other companies.

SKF gives top priority to the health and safety of employees, contractors, agency workers and visitors and is committed to providing safe 
and healthy working conditions to prevent work-related injury and ill health as well as to assure well-being in the work environment.

Workplace Safety at SKF India is guided by “SKF Care” principles for employees and “Business 
Care” and our operations are compliant with ISO 45001 standards

We work towards positively impacting environment by focusing on reducing energy 
consumption and carbon footprint.

SKF India is adopting various strategies for reducing its own 
operational carbon footprint as detailed in Principle 6.

Supply chain 
sustainability is very 
significant for resilient 
businesses. It is imperative to align the suppliers with the organisation’s sustainability vision for creating long-term value.

SKF India works closely with its suppliers for meeting the Sustainability targets. We also run a supplier ESG improvement 
programme wherein the ESG performance of the suppliers is mapped and measures are taken for implementing key 
actions.
Businesses care about meeting the customer expectations along with profitability and shareholder returns while adhering to the highest standards of ethical behaviour.

The Company has 
a strong code of conduct (CoC) in place outlining the strategies for transparent and accountable operations including Fair Competition Directive and Anti-Bribery and 
Anti-Corruption Directive to prevent, reduce and mitigate risk related to anti- corruption. 
All stakeholders are sensitised on our CoC and the Company has zero tolerance towards the non-compliance of CoC.

At SKF India, a ‘zero-tolerance’ approach prohibits bribery, improper payments, and gifts to government officials.

The organization has processes to prevent and manage conflicts of interest involving board members, including a Code of Conduct for Directors/Senior Management that outlines ethical practices. Any potential conflicts should be promptly reported to the Board for review and resolution.

Various capital investments targeting the improvement in social and environmental performance have been taken up in FY 2023-24 which includes Solar Captive Energy, Oil recycling, Safety, water management, Waste management

At SKF India, we actively promote recycling of scrap / rejected items by sending them to steel suppliers for repurposing. We also establish procedures for reclaiming products, offer remanufacturing services for select bearings, and provide comprehensive health monitoring and predictive maintenance for rotating equipment.

Our Code of Conduct demonstrates our commitment to providing equal opportunities for individuals with disabilities, ensuring inclusivity and non-discrimination in all aspects of employment. We prioritize inclusive development through fair employment practices and prohibit discrimination or harassment based on any grounds.

SKF prioritizes open communication, upholds gender equality, and has comprehensive policies to address ethics and grievances. All facilities have Internal Complaint Committees as mandated by the Prevention of Sexual Harassment Act. Employees and stakeholders can report grievances to HR or even directly to senior management. Disciplinary action is taken against Code of Conduct violators, and further details on child labor and anti-discrimination measures are available in Principle 5 of this report.

SKF is committed to achieving zero work-related accidents by prioritizing safety and preventing injuries. As an ISO 14001:2015 and ISO 45001:2018 certified Company, SKF India adheres to strict Environmental Management Systems (EMS) and Environmental, Health & Safety (EHS) protocols. The company conducts annual Management Review meetings and prioritizes occupant safety through comprehensive training programs. While the company experienced three recordable accidents this year, corrective actions have been taken. SKF is intensifying efforts towards achieving the zero-accident vision through ongoing engagement and awareness programs.

SKF India uses a Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment methodology to identify safety hazards and develop mitigation plans. They are prepared for emergencies like fire, spills, accidents, and natural disasters, and conduct regular mock drills and third-party audits to improve their risk management framework.
In FY 2024-25, SKF India prioritized employee well-being through regular training programs, awareness sessions, and additional safeguards and policies to ensure a safe and healthy work environment.

SKF India values trust and engagement with all stakeholder groups, supporting business goals and sustainable development. The company has a corporate sustainability focus, engaging with internal and external stakeholders through a systematic process to foster trust and growth.

SKF India has various channels for employees to raise discrimination grievances, including approaching HR, the Factory Manager, or the Director HR, individually or through the union. The company's responsible sourcing team works to address social risks in the supply chain and ensure ethical standards for all workers.

SKF India promotes fairness, equal opportunity, and gender parity in the workplace. It encourages employees to report any grievances or social risks, and it prioritizes whistleblower protection. Multiple reporting channels are available, and concerns are escalated for investigation and appropriate action.

Yes, SKF has implemented a Zero Liquid Discharge (ZLD) mechanism to prioritize environmental sustainability. Effluent Treatment Plants (ETPs) and Sewage Treatment Plants (STPs) are installed at all our units to treat wastewater effectively, and the treated water is repurposed for gardening

We are committed to achieving Net Zero emissions for our manufacturing sites by 2030. To reach this goal, we are transitioning to renewable energy and implementing energy efficiency projects to reduce our carbon footprint and support global sustainability efforts.

We are fully committed to waste management and sustainability. We ensure 100% recycling of non-hazardous waste and strictly adhere to regulations for hazardous waste. Our goal is to minimize environmental impact and promote a circular economy. Last year, we collected and recycled over 450 metric tons of plastic waste from our product packaging, showing our dedication to environmental stewardship and our proactive approach to addressing plastic pollution..

SKF India leverages customer feedback to continuously improve products and services. We have a mechanism to collect customer complaints in the form of Technical, Sales & Delivery related complaints Customer care, and a website for customers to raise their concerns. SKF prioritizes timely resolution of all customer complaints and inquiries.
